name: Enhance /cosmo:review: precise greenness, flake/unrelated-red lane, scoped DoD overrides
description: Enhance shared /cosmo:review: define 'green' as required-checks + verdict-comment (not all-checks/check-colour), add a flake/unrelated-red disposition, and read structured scoped DoD overrides from the WI/lane (not the reviewer's prompt). Surfaced from PRG-06 run-smoke; estate-level (zdx-marketplace).
acceptanceCriteria: All three drafts implemented in /cosmo:review (zdx-marketplace plugin), each with test/evidence: (1) DoD greenness = every required branch-protection check SUCCESS + a clean review verdict-comment; non-required and demonstrably-unrelated reds are non-gating; (2) explicit unrelated/flake disposition that notes (optionally files an infra WI) rather than bounces; (3) structured scoped DoD overrides read from the WI page / lane policy ({wi|workstream, rule_id, reason, approved_by, expires_at}), replacing free-text prompt overrides. Operator sign-off before shipping to the shared plugin; no regression to existing close behavior.
riskImpact: Estate-wide review-quality change — affects every workstream's close gate. Net safer, not more permissive: defining green as required-checks + verdict prevents both false bounces (non-required reds) and false closes (green-check-but-CHANGES_REQUESTED verdict). Ship behind operator sign-off; roll out via a zdx-marketplace version bump.
bodyText:
Context
Surfaced from PRG-06 (Identity Cutover) → WI-765 / PR #1183: a red non-required run-smoke check on a PR whose 4 required checks + claude-review were green. /cosmo:review hard-gates only the Cosmo-decidable mechanical DoD; "CI green / PR merged / AC met / symptom gone" are surfaced as a manual checklist the reviewer confirms by judgment. "CI green" is left undefined — so an agnostic reviewer can bounce good work on a non-required red, and a lane-specific exception has no auditable home.
Estate-level: lands in the shared cosmo plugin (zdx-marketplace), consumed by every workstream/project → operator sign-off before shipping.
(Note: WI-765 itself closed clean — the live reviewer correctly did not bounce on run-smoke — so this is a robustness/correctness improvement, not an urgent fix.)
Draft 1 — Define "green" precisely: required checks + review verdict (not "all checks" / check-colour)
Today: "verify PR merged / CI green if applicable" — undefined; reviewer judges.
Proposed: green for DoD holds only when (a) every required branch-protection check = SUCCESS and (b) the code-review verdict is clean (APPROVED, not CHANGES_REQUESTED). A red non-required check is not by itself a bounce reason — resolve required-vs-optional (gh api .../branches/<base>/protection/required_status_checks), do not ask "is anything red". Read the verdict from the claude[bot] issue comment (/issues/N/comments), allowing a beat for it to post — not the check colour, not /pulls/N/reviews.
Evidence: PR #888 (green check, CHANGES_REQUESTED verdict); WI-723 (verdict is a late issue-comment, missed by the reviews endpoint). [review-loop-observations.md]
Draft 2 — Add an explicit "unrelated / flake red" disposition
Today: no lane for "red but not your fault" → conservative reviewer bounces good work.
Proposed: when a red check is non-required OR demonstrably unrelated to the diff (infra flake, pre-existing failure on an unaffected surface), the reviewer notes it (optionally files/links a separate infra WI) and does not bounce on it. Separate "fix your PR" from "ambient infra".
Evidence: PR #902 (mobile timing flake on an API-only diff). [review-loop-observations.md]
Draft 3 — Read scoped DoD overrides from the work item / lane policy, not the prompt
Today: lane exceptions (e.g. the WI-585/586 WP-children waiver) are hand-injected into the reviewer's prompt text — not auditable, lost on re-spawn, unreachable for a context-agnostic reviewer.
Proposed: /cosmo:review reads a structured, scoped override — {wi|workstream, rule_id, reason, approved_by, expires_at} — from where the command already reads (the WI page or a lane policy file). E.g. "ignore run-smoke for Identity Cutover until the cutover re-run". Overrides become auditable + expirable; an agnostic reviewer applies them with no briefing.
Evidence: reviewer-observations 2026-06-11/12 — "model overrides as structured scoped policy, not free-text prompt". [review-loop-reviewer-observations.md]
Possible Draft 4 (under discussion — reviewer observation-capture)
The reviewer has no durable protocol by design (its process is this skill), so any standing reviewer behavior must live here, not in the per-spawn kickoff. Fold in if approved: (a) emit a structured review-result JSON per review (disposition, blocking_dod_rules, evidence_urls, transition_key, timings) for orchestrator harvest; (b) a light step — "append a dated one-liner to the lane's review-observations log on any loop-improving anomaly". Both automatic for every reviewer, with zero kickoff thickening.
Provenance
Drafted by the orchestrator from the PRG-06 run-smoke adjudication (shepherd outbox prg06ic-002) + the review-loop PoC observation files. Estate-level change vehicle: zdx-marketplace cosmo plugin, not this repo.
Handover notes for PRG-05 (capture only — DECIDE/DRAFT in the PRG-05 context, not in the PRG-06 session that filed this)
This WI surfaced during PRG-06 but is PRG-05 (machinery) work. Captured here so PRG-05 picks it up cold without re-deriving. No design decisions or drafting are made in the PRG-06 context — the items below marked OPEN are for PRG-05 to decide with its own context + your sign-off.
Scoping (from reading review.ts + SKILL.md, so PRG-05 needn't redo it): review.ts does ONLY the mechanical, Cosmo-decidable DoD (runDod in dod.ts → summary / Fixed-In / dates / AC / WP-bulk-ready) and applies the transition. It NEVER evaluates CI/PR/checks — those are surfaced as a manual checklist for the reviewer's judgment (SKILL.md step 2 runs gh by hand). Therefore:
Drafts 1 & 2 (green-definition, flake lane) = SKILL.md guidance + a wording tweak to the manual-checklist text. No gating-logic change; low-risk. Run-smoke handling lives HERE (reviewer judgment), NOT in scoped overrides.
Draft 3 (scoped overrides) = the only mechanical-gate CODE change: dod.ts reads a structured override and drops the waived rule_ids before computing mechanicalOk. Canonical case = the WI-585/586 WP-children waiver (a real mechanical rule).
OPEN DESIGN QUESTION (defer to PRG-05): where do scoped overrides live? (a) a WI-page property — per-WI, auditable in Cosmo, but adds a field to the estate Work-Items schema; or (b) a lane policy file the skill reads — no schema change, another artifact. Override record shape: {rule_id, reason, approved_by, expires_at, scope}.
Change vehicle: the shared zdx-marketplace cosmo plugin (not this repo). Ship behind operator sign-off; do NOT swap /cosmo:review while any lane's reviews are in flight.
CORRECTION — the "Possible Draft 4" above is RETRACTED. Reviewer observation-capture is OSER machinery and belongs in the reviewer's OSER layer (kickoff-template + watcher), NOT in this generic cross-ZDX skill. It is tracked under the OSER meta-eye work, not WI-770.
